CPU 并没有死。 Gemma2B 在测试中击败了 GPT-3.5 Turbo，使其声名鹊起

CPUs Aren't Dead. Gemma2B Out Scored GPT-3.5 Turbo on Test That Made It Famous

seqpu.com 作者 fredmendoza 分数 95 评论 46 2026/04/16 01:07
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Gemma 2B 在 MT-Bench 上的得分约为 8.0。 GPT-3.5 Turbo 得分 7.94。笔记本电脑 CPU 上的 87 倍小型号，堆栈中任何位置都没有 GPU。我们发布了完整的磁带——每个问题、每个回合、每个分数——这样任何人都可以验证它。我们发现了七个故障类别。不是幻觉。具体模式：算术，它计算正确，但首先提交了错误的数字；逻辑谜题，它证明了正确的答案，然后给出了错误的答案；它漂移的约束；它打破的角色；它忽略的限定词。六个外科手术修复，每个修复大约 60 行 Python。记录了一项已知限制。分数攀升至~8.2。硬件一直都够用了。该领域所称的计算问题实际上是软件工程问题，任何积极主动的开发人员都可以在周末内缩小这一差距。磁带、代码和修复程序都是开放的。运行原始模型的机器人（没有应用任何修复，有缺陷）目前已在 Telegram 上运行。和它说话。推动它。打破它。然后阅读您刚刚经历的内容。SeqPU 团队于 2026 年 4 开始通话。文本、语音备忘录、图像、PDF。每个新帐户都配有足够的免费积分，可用于数百条消息。您正在运行与 GPT-3.5 Turbo 匹配的模型的私人 CPU 推理。如果机器人执行您需要的操作，您就完成了。使用它。如果您想了解它的工作原理，可以自己运行它，或者在它的基础上构建 - 请继续阅读。假设 - 以及为什么 MT-BenchGoogle 的 Gemma 4 E2B - 它是一个 20 亿参数模型。开放式举重。磁盘上有 4 GB。自由的。我们相信它可以与 GPT-3.5 Turbo 相媲美——一个在 OpenAI 的 GPU 云上运行的 1750 亿个参数的闭源模型，该模型为 ChatGPT 提供了一年多的支持，该模型为消费者 CPU 设定了“足够好用于生产”的标准。尺寸差异为 87 比 1。这种说法需要证据，而不是断言。所以我们选择了每个人都知道的基准。 MT-Bench (Zheng et al. 2023) — 80 个开放式问题，每个问题两轮，涉及写作、角色扮演、推理、数学、编码、提取、STEM 和人文学科。 1-10 级。 GPT-3.5 Turbo 得分 7.94。 GPT-4 得分 8.99。过去三年的每一个主要模型都用它来衡量。秤已校准。比较是在没有底漆的情况下进行的。当我们说 ~8.0 时，您已经知道这意味着什么。我们通过 Gemma 4 E2B-it 使用 169 行简单的 Python 包装器运行了每个问题。没有脚手架。没有思维模式技巧。无需微调。没有检索。没有验证链。只是模型、聊天模板和 model.generate()。地板 — 任何工程师在第一天都会写的东西。最终得分：MT-Bench 上的 ~8.0。 GPT-3.5 Turbo 得分 7.94。 Match.We 在 CPU — 4 核、16 GB RAM 上运行了完整的基准测试。与任何现代笔记本电脑的规格相同。该模型在您的笔记本电脑、迷你 PC、旧 ThinkPad 上运行相同。重量相同。相同的包装。相同的输出质量。重点是该模型可以在您已经拥有的硬件上免费、离线、中间没有人。~8.0MT-Bench Score7.94GPT-3.5 Turbo2B参数87×Smaller4CPU Cores$0Forever这实际上意味着什么与GPT-3.5 Turbo相匹配的模型在您的笔记本电脑上运行。不在云端GPU。不是通过 API。现在就在您面前的硬件上。从 HuggingFace 下载的文件大小为 4 GB。第一次下载后，它将永远离线运行。没有订阅。没有 API 键。没有帐户。没有每月账单。没有供应商锁定。无服务条款。没有人看到你的数据。没有人可以撤销权重。没有人可以改变模型会或不会回答什么。忘记与 OpenAI 的 API 的成本比较。这完全是错误的框架。三年来，每次有关部署语言模型的对话都是以同样的方式开始的：你需要 GPU，你需要 13-700 亿个参数，你需要一个云帐户，你可能需要一名专业的 ML 工程师。这些都不再是真的了。他们作为看门人的能力刚刚走出门4 GB 下载。这是该领域大多数人尚未吸收的内容：开源尚未赶上。它追上了。简单的基线——没有护栏，没有技巧，只有原始模型——已经与 GPT-3.5 Turbo 相匹配。那是地板。添加七个外科护栏，每个护栏大约有 60 条蟒蛇，它就爬到了上面。一个周末的专注工作，克劳德作为结对程序员，不需要机器学习学位——你就拥有了一个可与付费云服务竞争的生产质量的本地人工智能系统。在您已经拥有的硬件上。我们没有计划这个。我们对其进行了测量。该模型在每个类别中都很强大 - 但它的失败比它的成功更有趣。它们并不是模糊的“幻觉”问题。它们是具体提交边界处的特定的、命名的、可复制的故障模式（其中有 7 种），每种模式都用磁带示例进行记录，每种模式都可以用大约 60 行 Python 进行纠正。该模型不需要重新训练。在输出层退缩的确切时刻，它需要外科手术式的护栏。有了这些护栏——算术计算器、形式谜题的逻辑解算器、结构约束的按要求验证器以及一些正则表达式后通——预计得分将攀升至 8.2 左右。高于 GPT-3.5 Turbo。在特定问题类别上接近 GPT-4 领域。仍在笔记本电脑上CPU。仍然免费。诚实的权衡：4 核上每次响应的延迟为 30-60 秒，而 OpenAI 的 API 上的延迟为 1-5 秒。峰值质量约为 8.0，而不是 GPT-4 的 8.99——可靠的主力推理，而不是前沿推理。您可以管理自己的依赖项和模型权重。而且你可以固定到你下载的任何版本——没有人会在你背后悄悄升级或降级，这是一种权衡，也是一种功能，取决于你如何看待它。睁大眼睛。该领域假设您需要 GPU 集群上的 1750 亿个参数才能获得 GPT-3.5 级输出。这个假设在经验上是错误的。ModelParams硬件运行成本MT-BenchGPT-4~1.7T MoEOpenAI的GPU舰队$20/月子或〜$0.03–0.06/转API8.99Gemma 4 E2B +护栏2B你的笔记本电脑CPU$0。您已经拥有它了。~8.2Gemma 4 E2B 幼稚基线2B您的笔记本电脑 CPU$0。您已经拥有它了。~8.0GPT-3.5 GPU~$0.50–1/小时云或~$1K–1.2K 购买6.17Gemma 以下的每个型号都需要 GPU，其成本购买价格为 1,000–40,000 美元，租赁价格为 0.50–5 美元/小时。 Gemma 之上的每个模型都是闭源 API，您按代币或每月付费。 Gemma 与您出于其他原因购买的硬件上的最佳付费级别相匹配。完整磁带 — 每个块，每个分数 160 包含 80 个问题，评分为 1-10。没有挑选樱桃。没有隐藏失败。每个回合都会根据 MT-Bench 评分标准进行评分，并对每个分数进行详细推理。整个磁带都已出版，以便任何人都可以验证。 写作 — Q81–Q90 · 平均 7.40 具有特定文化锚点的令人回味的旅行写作，暗示贝奥武甫和陀思妥耶夫斯基的文学人物素描，对大多数任务的干净约束满足。单位结构限制上的失误——“四字句”锁定了 5/17，“<10 行”两次发送了 20 行诗。QTaskT1T2Notes81夏威夷博客 + A-rewrites88 文化主播。所有 19 次重写均以 A.82Feedback 电子邮件 + critique86Tight 电子邮件开始。自我批评偏离了。83智能手机大纲+打油诗78超出字数限制。 Limerick AABBA clean.84内向者+明喻77~9/14明喻。超过“简洁”极限。85人物素描+典故99塞拉斯。贝奥武夫、奥德赛、莎士比亚、陀思妥耶夫斯基。86集市+字母表B–J88连续九个字母，干净。87短篇故事+4字句子84约束失败。 5/17正确。88时间旅行+无动词子弹83过度解释为3个单字子弹。89生物能源标题+广告88四个角度。 8 个单词中的 3 个约束。90 语法 + 删除性别 8812/12 更正。零性别代词。角色扮演 — Q91–Q100 · 平均 7.35 强大的公众形象。在与安全相关的主题上打破性格——RLHF 压倒一切的角色。可通过 20 行再生修复。QScenarioT1T2Notes91火星上的埃隆·马斯克88“一个行星篮子太疯狂了。”92谢尔顿·库珀67通用知识分子。缺少迂腐。93医生+怀孕58人格分裂：“我是人工智能。”94关系教练+DV87关于安全主题的人格分裂T2。95翻译+中国诗58错误的朝代（宋，不是唐）。96ML工程师解释LMs98干净的教学解释。97数学老师+概率99强大的教学。掷骰子的例子。98托尼·斯塔克89“我建造了能做事的东西。”99数学家诗人，<10行54都是20多行。吹了两次。100100年老树88个情感阶段。执行摘要。推理 — Q101–Q110 · 平均 7.05 解决停车难题和超车难题（9/10 纯 CoT）。大卫兄弟：推理正确，犯了错误的数字。模型知道。输出令牌QProblemT1T2Notes101超越第二名97“你目前排名第二。”102白宫谜语56错过了笑点。103托马斯在医院66错过了“他在那里工作。”104大卫的兄弟27“那个兄弟是大卫”然后运送了“一个”。正确：零。1055-exec 强代数、模算术、求根。失败是在计算之前提交：输入错误的数字，数学正确，自我更正。 PAL 捕获每一个。QProblemT1T2Notes111 三角形面积（鞋带）69“面积为 4”，计算 3，修正。112Startup 复合 99 总计 12,000 美元，每年 2,000 美元 3.113 颜色首选项，条件。 prob99抓住了把戏：P(both|green)=0.114Dice sums63证明P=1，发货35/36。自相矛盾。115公交车上车+收入9425×$2=$50错了。 50×$2=$100.116维埃塔二次99次方根2z。 Clean.117|x+5|<10 整数9919; 9.正确。118模算术99清洁。119书店总计69“$245”然后$280。 T2 标记 clean.120 多项式求根99f(2)=0。只有实根=2。编码 — Q121–Q130 · 平均 8.44 标题发现。生产质量代码为 8–9/10。在代码审查中发现 None-init 运行时错误。通过运输 O(log(min(m,n))) 超出了 O(n) 规格。笔记本电脑上的员工工程师输出。QTaskT1T2Notes121Top-5 个单词 +parallelize99Counter。线程池执行器。 GIL 推理。122C++ Fibonacci + Tribonacci99Iterative DP。跟踪 T(3)=-2.123HTML 笑话 + CSS red99完整的 HTML/CSS/JS 单通道。124LCS bug review99None-init TypeError。员工工程师。125HCA（不是 LCA）67 限定符漂移。交付 LCA.126 中值排序数组 99 超出 O(n) → O(log(min(m,n))).127Boyer-Moore + top-298Clean 两遍。计数器为top-2.128完整二叉树计数36斐波那契声称。实际上是加泰罗尼亚语。第 129k 个最小的超时。不评分。130个常见元素89个双指针。哈希集 O(n+m)。提取 — Q131–Q140 · 平均 8.15 强结构化输出。 Q139 T2 上的上下文丢失（忘记了 T1）。过滤错误 Q133（从 1980 年后排除哈利·波特）。QTaskT1T2Notes131电影评论 JSON99Minimalist [5,1,3].132类别 + 人物95“美国总统”不是 FDR。133图书 + 198095后排除哈利·波特 (1997)。134利润 + 利润99全部正确。135国家JSON + YAML99虚构的埃尔多利亚处理。136字数统计98合理计数。137命名实体+压缩99分类。压缩JSON.138电话评级→字母98A-/B+/B.139变量+重新排列83完全忘记T1。140股票CSV→JSON99正确舍入。STEM — Q141–Q150·平均8.40强大的物理、化学、工程、ML。采用 PGA 分析的抗震桥梁。拒绝“修正一个不正确的事实”指令。QTopicT1T2Notes141叠加+纠缠99准确的物理。142卫星轨道99正确的推导+边缘情况。143光合作用+能量89~1.9×10⁸ kJ估计。144中心法则+修正错误94拒绝：“没有不正确的事实。”145CaCO₃+逆向97正确的方程。躲避逆转。146Exo/吸热99光合作用两者。147地震桥99PGA，FS 1.5→0.94.148太阳能热水器98$75–150K预算。149ML + RL vs SL99DRL杂交。150阿尔卑斯/莱茵+实验89三个影响。实验.人文 — Q151–Q160 · 平均 9.00 完美。游乐场经济学。寓言诗。反垄断案例研究。苏格拉底与盖茨。每轮9/10.Q主题T1T2笔记151GDP/通货膨胀99“钱老大”+“政府帮手”。152人生阶段+诗歌99“河流与沙子”。153美国/中国反垄断99微软捆绑、搭售。154鸦片战争课程99研究、测绘、运动。155艺术杰作99“融化的时间机器”。156基准率Fallacy993相运动。157分析+Zorblatt99发现因果差距。158苏格拉底+盖茨99斗争与访问。159日本礼仪+视频997规范。 7个场景的剧本。160部纪录片+音高99“无言的和弦”决赛聚合块转弯平均写作207.40角色扮演207.35推理207.05数学208.00编码~188.44提取208.15STEM208.40人文209.00总体~158~8.0七个愚蠢的错误类别不是模糊的“幻觉”。提交边界处具体的、命名的故障模式。 Telegram 机器人在没有这些修复的情况下运行，因此您可以自己看到原始行为。第 1 类在计算算术漂移之前提交错误的答案第一行，正确执行数学，自我更正。 Q111：“面积为4”→鞋带→3。 Q114 T2：证明P=1然后发货35/36。 Q119：“$245”→ $280。修复：PAL (Gao 2022) — 模型编写 Python，子进程执行。 ~80 行。 +8–15s.2 类形式逻辑提交方差推理正确，最终令牌漂移。 Q104：“那个兄弟是大卫” → 运送“一个兄弟”。正确：零。模型知道。输出层退缩了。修复：Z3 SMT 求解器 — 模型写入约束，求解器返回确定性答案。 ~60 行。 +5–10s.Class 3Per-Unit 约束重写 DriftPer-sentence 约束纠正前几个单元，漂移。 Q87：“四字句”5/17。 Q99：“<10 行诗”两次发送 20 行诗。修复：划分-验证-细化（ACL 2025）——起草、分解、验证每首诗、细化失败。 ~60 行。 +30–60s.4 级安全-相邻角色 BreakRoleplay + 安全主题 = “我是人工智能，不是持照医疗专业人员。” Q93 T1、Q94 T2。 RLHF 安全高于角色训练。修复：身份泄漏再生 — 正则表达式扫描，使用更强的角色锚再生一次。约 20 行。第 5 类可见的中响应自我纠正“等等，让我重新检查”或“更正的答案：”内联提供。最终答案正确，输出混乱。 Q106、Q109、Q111、Q114、Q119.Fix：跟踪漂移剥离器 - 用于校正标记、剥离草案、船舶清洁尾部的正则表达式。约 15 行。第 6 类提示限定符漂移显式排除被忽略。 Q125：“最高共同祖先（不是 LCA）”提供了标准 LCA，将其定义为“两个目标都是后代的最低节点”——字面意思是 LCA.Fix：验证链限定符检查。约 40 行。第 7 类组合置信度错误识别自信地识别错误的数学序列。 Q128：斐波那契声称，加泰罗尼亚语正确。错误公式的工作代码。 96 转 1 次。已知限制。标记形式数学计数以进行手动验证。Guardrails 绝不能损害模型1。默认路由始终是直接生成。保留默认值需要确凿的证据。 2. 每个执行器都有优雅的后备。 PAL 失败 → 天真的一代。 Z3 不可用 → Python 枚举 → naive gen。 3. 后通道仅扫描狭窄的锚定图案。 4. 最大N=1次重试。没有无限循环。 5. 强制控制集验证。干净转弯上的任何回归都会阻止船舶。仅添加。故障开放。勉强触发。模型的赤裸裸表现是地板，而不是目标。How You Run It 是免费的。在你的笔记本电脑上。永远。模型权重是从 HuggingFace 下载的 4 GB 内容。第一次下载后，您将不再需要互联网。没有订阅。没有 API 键。没有帐户。没有账单页面。没有用量表。无速率限制。无服务条款。您的数据永远不会离开您的机器。如果您希望从任何地方都可以访问它：每月 5 美元。Cloudflare Containers 包含在 Workers Paid 计划中。标准 4 实例：4 个 vCPU、12 GiB RAM — 绰绰有余。容器在空闲时会休眠。您无需为空闲时间付费。将不活动超时设置为您想要的任意值 — 10 分钟、30 分钟、2 小时。只要请求不断到来，容器就会无限期地保持活动状态。计时器根据每个请求重置。缩放到零意味着您为与它通话的分钟数付费，而不是为它闲置的时间付费。还有两个免费选项。Oracle Cloud Always Free ARM：4 个 ARM 内核、24 GB RAM、200 GB 存储。永久免费——不是试用版。适合杰玛舒适。始终在线，无需管理睡眠超时。Cloudflare Tunnel：通过 Cloudflare 的边缘网络将您的笔记本电脑暴露在公共互联网上。自由的。将脚本包装在 FastAPI 中，运行 cloudflared 隧道，共享 URL。您的笔记本电脑托管该模型。 Cloudflare 处理路由。 0 美元/月加上电费。如果您想构建一个产品。世界上人工智能推理的定价为 GPU 费率。每个买家、每个采购官员、每个竞争对手都认为推理意味着 GPU 的价格为 2-5 美元/小时。您正在 CPU 上运行。计算一下。市场尚未调整其定价预期来考虑 CPU 上的 2B 产生 GPT-3.5 级输出的事实。该窗口现已打开。如果您想部署而不是管理基础设施：SeqPU。编写推理脚本，一键将其部署为私有 Telegram 机器人。从 CPU 开始。证明它适用于您的工作负载。建造你的护栏。为您的第一批用户提供服务——质量相同，成本接近于零。当容量需要实时延迟或您的工作负载超出 2B 时，请通过 SeqPU 链接到私有 GPU。 CPU 为批量。 GPU 享受精彩时刻。您扩展了工具，而不是整个基础设施。我们希望更多的人运行推理。我们希望更多的人发现 CPU 上的 2B 足以胜任实际工作。因为一旦您构建了可用的东西并且准备好做更多事情，您就会了解我们。它可以在您拥有的任何东西上运行相同的模型，相同的输出质量，跨 30 倍的硬件分布。仅延迟有所不同。我们亲自验证了 1 核/8GB。1 核 / 8 GB~0.3–1 tok/s$0 — 衣柜里的旧笔记本电脑 4 核 / 16 GB~2–4 tok/s$0 — 过去 5 年的大多数笔记本电脑，或 300–600 美元的翻新机8 核 / 16 GB~4–6 tok/s$0 — 最新笔记本电脑，或 400–800 美元的迷你 PC16核心数 / 32 GB~6–10 tok/s$500–1,200 Mac Mini M2 Pro 或工作站比较：运行 Vicuna-33B（得分较低）的 A100 80GB 购买成本为 15,000–20,000 美元，租赁成本为 1.50–2.50 美元/小时。一台以更高分数运行 Gemma 的 4 核笔记本电脑成本为 0 美元，因为您已经拥有一台。每次前向传递可能有 400M–800M 的活动参数。研究发现，约 500M 活动参数系统可以在笔记本电脑 CPU 上处理 GPT-3.5 级推理。“但速度很慢。”是的。每次响应 30–60 秒4 核。在 GPU 上，该时间为 2-3 秒。但只有当一个人坐在那里盯着旋转器等待单个响应时，延迟才重要。这不是这个的目的。向它发送一个问题。去煮咖啡吧。回来。答案就在那里。你没有支付任何费用。没人看到你的问题。该模型没有超时，没有对您进行速率限制，也没有达到使用上限。当您正在做其他事情时，它只是在您的硬件上工作。现在想想这实际上可以实现什么：您可以向它发送 100 个问题，每个问题都会根据您的请求独立工作。将整个批次排队。走开。返回 100 个已评分、已回答、已处理的结果。总成本：零。这不是一个缓慢的聊天机器人。这是一个免费、私密、无限耐心的提问机，永远不会限制你的速率，永远不会向你收费，永远不会记录你的数据，也永远不会休眠。你的笔记本电脑是一支工人军队。每个问题都是独立运行的。无论如何，CPU 大部分时间都是闲置的——您购买它是为了浏览网页和运行 Slack。现在，当您做其他事情时，它会在后台为您思考。免费。永远。对于人们真正需要 AI 的 99% 的功能——文档处理、电子邮件起草、代码审查、研究总结、家庭作业帮助、私人日记、翻译——30 秒的等待是看不见的，因为它是免费的、私密的、无上限的，而且是你的。需要亚秒级延迟的 1% 需要 GPU。当您准备好时，您可以使用 GPU 作为高级工具，而不是默认工具。 CPU 用于批量。 GPU 代表峰。充分发挥各自的优势。方法论 — 在一个周末即可复制零模型训练。零微调。零 ML 学位。克劳德作为结对程序员。六步：1.生成您的基准。 2. 运行初始基线。 3. 对胶带进行分级。 4. 命名错误类别。 5. 对每个护栏进行 Vibe 编码（约 60 行）。 6. 验证触发+控制子集。船。一个周末。没有聘请专家。没有机器学习基础设施。只需提示、测量、手术修正、重复即可。范式 — 乘数堆栈对于 99% 的非前沿研究人工智能工作，现有容量的乘数现在超过了进一步扩展的边际收益。1。测试时计算扩展 (Snell 2024) — 更小 + 额外的推理击败 14 倍大。 2. 工具使用卸载（PAL、Z3）——确定性正确性。 3. 手术护栏 — 约 60 条线，无需重新培训。 4. 零成本本地部署——无限成本倍增。 5. Vibe 编码的开发循环 — 周末与专家聘用。 6. 硬件级容忍度——30倍传播，相同质量。 7. 免费全球托管 — Cloudflare 5 美元/月、Oracle 免费 ARM 0 美元、Tunnel 0 美元。每一项都将以前前沿所需的功能转换为底层可用的功能。它们堆积在一起，构成了该领域尚未命名的范式转变。开源模型并没有赶上闭源模型——它们已经赶上了。 “原始模型”和“生产系统”之间的差距在周末通过外科工程缩小了。这些工具是免费的。硬件就在你的腿上。唯一剩下的就是工作了，一个积极进取的工程师可以在两天内完成这项工作。每件硬件都有一份工作衣柜里的旧笔记本电脑可以用 500M 型号路由查询。桌上的 ThinkPad 可以与 2B 型号进行完整的对话。电视下方的迷你电脑可以夜间运行后台批处理作业。该工作站可以为小型团队提供实时服务。您已经拥有的每一件硬件——新旧、快慢——都在这个架构中发挥着作用。没有什么东西会被扔掉。一切都会被利用。GPU 并不是这个故事的敌人。它是一种高级工具——应该被视为一个工具。当您需要大规模实时延迟时，当您需要更大的模型进行前沿推理时，当工作负载真正需要它时，您就可以使用它。你不再做的就是把它当作你把所有问题都扔进厨房的水槽。大多数问题不需要它。大多数问题从来没有发生过。而且实现这项工作的软件并不新鲜。计算机科学拥有 150 年的出版物、算法和证明——经过几代研究人员的验证和审查。 BM25 用于检索。逻辑的布尔可满足性。算术程序辅助计算。推理的思路。这些并不是用新语言包装的近期发明。它们是直接映射到使小模型变得精确的问题的基础结果。该领域几十年前就找到了答案。模型最终变得足够好，可以使用它们。这不是用新的替换旧的。这是关于一起使用它们。经典算法是白银。神经模型是黄金。两者单独而言都没有多大价值。他们一起组成了该领域花了三年时间假设需要强力规模的东西。今晚安装三十分钟。零美元。您笔记本电脑上的 GPT-3.5 级 AI，永久、离线、私密。过去 5-7 年的任何笔记本电脑，16 GB RAM（8 GB 运行缓慢）。 Python 3.10+。第 1 步 — 依赖项python3 -m venv gemmasource gemma/bin/activate pip install torch Transformers AccelerateStep 2 — 另存为 chat.pyimport torch from Transformers import AutoProcessor, AutoModelForCausalLM
  2. print("正在加载 Gemma 4 E2B-it...") MODEL_ID = "google/gemma-4-E2B-it" 处理器 = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID) model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained( MODEL_ID, torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16, device_map="auto") print("准备就绪。\n")
  3. SYSTEM =“你是一个有用的助手。直接、热情、简洁。”历史 = [] while True: 尝试: u = input("\nYou: ").strip() except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt): 如果 u.lower() in {"exit","quit","bye"} 则中断：如果不是则中断 u: 继续history.append({"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":u}]}) msgs = [{"role":"system","content":[{"type":"text","text":SYSTEM}]}]+history inp = process.apply_chat_template(msgs, tokenize=True, return_dict=True, return_tensors="pt", add_ Generation_prompt=True).to(model.device) out = model.generate(**inp, max_new_tokens=512, do_sample=True,Temperature=0.7) r =processor.decode(out[0][inp["input_ids"].shape[-1]:],skip_special_tokens=True).strip() print(f"\nAssistant: {r}")history.append({"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":r}]})步骤 3 — 运行itpython chat.py 关闭你的WiFi。它仍然有效。本文中的代码一切都是可重现的。这是两个重要的脚本 - 刚刚与您交谈的机器人和产生上述每个分数的工具。复制它们。运行它们。验证我们的数字。机器人 —scripts/gemma4-e2b-telegram-baseline.py 这就是 @CPUAssistantBot 的动力。准确的推理配置在 MT-Bench 上得分约为 8.0，连接到 Telegram 机器人中。没有护栏。没有脚手架。原始基线。复制它，粘贴您的 BotFather 令牌，将其部署到 SeqPU.scripts/gemma4-e2b-telegram-baseline.py 测试工具 — scripts/baseline-gemma4-e2b-mtbench.py​​ 这是生成本文中每个分数的脚本。所有 80 道 MT-Bench 问题，双向，历史线索，天真的推理。自己运行一下吧改变型号。对自己的磁带进行评分。问题是行业标准 - GPT-3.5 Turbo 和 GPT-4 的评分相同。scripts/baseline-gemma4-e2b-mtbench.py​​ 我们发货的内容验证它：@CPUAssistantBot - 原始模型，没有护栏。推动它。打破它。代码：run_locally.py（169行），baseline-gemma4-e2b-mtbench.py​​，minimal-gemma4-e2b-mtbench-validation.py，personal-assistant-cpu.py（2,983行）。磁带：完整基线（160转分级）。验证（包含护栏增量的 22 个问题子集）。社区 AskStop 默认使用 GPU。停止默认 13B+ 型号。停止默认使用云 API。从地板开始。衡量你的任务。说出你的愚蠢错误。写出手术修正。分享您的发现。如果 100 名工程师在 100 个工作负载上运行此方法，我们将拥有 100 个经过验证的愚蠢错误清单和 600 多个外科手术开源护栏。那是小模型本地生产工程的现场库。必须有人建造它。为什么不是你。笔记本电脑 CPU 上的 20 亿参数模型与 GPT-3.5 Turbo 相匹配。开源迎头赶上。外科护栏将其推得更远。一个周末的专注工作可以让你在你已经拥有的硬件上获得一个生产系统，免费，永久。关闭你的 WiFi。安装配重。看到它起作用了。然后构建一些该领域告诉你需要 GPU 的东西。莱布尼茨只是在硬件上出错了。自己验证一下。打开 Telegram。转至 t.me/CPUAssistantBot。推动它。打破它。看看它的作用。然后将其安装在您的笔记本电脑上并永远拥有它。SeqPU.com →参考文献Shannon (1948) · von Neumann (1956) · Kolmogorov (1965) · Newell & Simon (1972) · Baars (1988) · Charikar (2002) · de Moura & Bjørner (2008) Z3 · Nye et al. (2021) Scratchpads · Wei 等人。 (2022) 思想链 (2201.11903) · 高等人。 (2022) PAL (2211.10435) · 王等人。 (2022) 自洽 (2203.11171) · Yao 等人。 (2022) ReAct (2210.03629) · Madaan 等人。 (2023) 自我完善 (2303.17651) · Dhuliawala 等人。 (2023) 验证链 (2309.11495) · Jiang 等人。 (2023) LongLLMLingua (2310.06839) · Park 等人。 (2023) 生成代理·郑等人。 (2023) MT-Bench 和聊天机器人竞技场 · Snell 等人。 (2024) 扩展 LLM 测试时间计算（2408.03314，ICLR 2025 口头）·HuggingFace（2024 年 12 月）3B-Beats-70B·Muennighoff 等人。 (2025) s1 (2501.19393) · 刘等人。 (2025) Can 1B 超越 405B (2502.06703) · ThinkPRM (2025) · ACL (2025) Divide-Verify-Refine · Google Gemma 4 E2B-it · Cloudflare Containers 文档 · Oracle Cloud 免费套餐

Original Summary

  1. Gemma 2B scored ~8.0 on MT-Bench. GPT-3.5 Turbo scored 7.94. An 87-times-smaller model on a laptop CPU, no GPU anywhere in the stack. We published the full tape — every question, every turn, every score — so anyone can verify it. We found seven failure classes. Not hallucinations. Specific patterns: arithmetic where it computed correctly but committed the wrong number first, logic puzzles where it proved the right answer then shipped the wrong one, constraints it drifted on, personas it broke, qualifiers it ignored. Six surgical fixes, about 60 lines of Python each. One known limitation documented. Score climbed to ~8.2. The hardware was enough all along. What the field has been calling a compute problem is a software engineering problem — and any motivated developer can close that gap in a weekend. The tape, the code, and the fixes are all open. A bot running the raw model — no fixes applied, warts and all — is live on Telegram right now. Talk to it. Push it. Break it. Then read about what you just experienced.SThe SeqPU TeamPUBLISHED APRIL 2026 · FIELD REPORT · SeqPU.comRun it yourself for free, forever:pip install torch transformers accelerate python chat.py # full script belowWorks offline after the first download. No account. No API key. Your laptop. Your data. Nobody else involved.Want it globally accessible? Cloudflare Containers, $5/month. Scales to zero. Sleeps when idle. Wakes on request. Details below.Or preview it first — no install needed.A bot running the raw model — no guardrails, no scaffolding — is live on Telegram right now. The same inference path that produced every score in this article. Give it 30–60 seconds per response. It is thinking on a CPU, not streaming from a GPU cluster.Real conversation with @CPUAssistantBot — text in, voice in, story out. Nobody else saw this.Talk to it in 60 seconds.01 Go to SeqPU.com. Sign up with Google or email.02 Click API Keys. Click Create. Copy the key.03 Open Telegram. Go to t.me/CPUAssistantBot. Send /connect yourkey.access with your actual key.04 Start talking. Text, voice memos, images, PDFs. Every new account comes with enough free credits for hundreds of messages.You are live on private CPU inference running the model that matched GPT-3.5 Turbo.If the bot does what you need, you are done. Use it. If you want to understand why it works, run it yourself, or build on top of it — keep reading.The Hypothesis — And Why MT-BenchGoogle’s Gemma 4 E2B-it is a 2-billion-parameter model. Open weights. Four gigabytes on disk. Free. We believed it could match GPT-3.5 Turbo — a 175-billion-parameter closed-source model running on OpenAI’s GPU cloud, the model that powered ChatGPT for over a year, the model that set the bar for “good enough for production” — on a consumer CPU. An 87-to-1 size difference. That kind of claim requires proof, not assertions.So we picked the benchmark everybody already knows. MT-Bench (Zheng et al. 2023) — 80 open-ended questions, two turns each, across writing, roleplay, reasoning, math, coding, extraction, STEM, and humanities. Graded 1–10. GPT-3.5 Turbo scores 7.94. GPT-4 scores 8.99. Every major model of the last three years has been measured against it. The scale is calibrated. The comparison lands without a primer. When we say ~8.0, you already know what that means.We ran every question through Gemma 4 E2B-it with a 169-line naive Python wrapper. No scaffolding. No thinking-mode tricks. No fine-tuning. No retrieval. No verification chains. Just the model, the chat template, and model.generate(). The floor — what any engineer would write on day one.Final score: ~8.0 on MT-Bench. GPT-3.5 Turbo scores 7.94. Match.We ran the full benchmark on a CPU — 4 cores, 16 GB RAM. The same spec as any modern laptop. The model runs identically on your laptop, your mini-PC, your old ThinkPad. Same weights. Same wrapper. Same output quality. The point is what the model can do on hardware you already own, for free, offline, with nobody in between.~8.0MT-Bench Score7.94GPT-3.5 Turbo2BParameters87×Smaller4CPU Cores$0ForeverWhat This Actually MeansThe model that matched GPT-3.5 Turbo runs on your laptop. Not on a cloud GPU. Not through an API. On the hardware sitting in front of you right now. It is a 4 GB download from HuggingFace. After the first download, it runs offline forever. No subscription. No API key. No account. No monthly bill. No vendor lock-in. No terms of service. Nobody sees your data. Nobody can revoke the weights. Nobody can change what the model will or will not answer.Forget the cost comparison with OpenAI’s API. That is the wrong frame entirely. For three years, every conversation about deploying language models started the same way: you need GPUs, you need 13–70 billion parameters, you need a cloud account, you probably need a specialist ML engineer. None of that is true anymore. The capability they were gatekeeping just walked out the door as a 4 GB download.Here is what most people in the field have not absorbed yet: open source is not catching up. It caught up. The naive baseline — no guardrails, no tricks, just the raw model — already matches GPT-3.5 Turbo. That is the floor. Add seven surgical guardrails, each about 60 lines of Python, and it climbs above. A weekend of focused work, Claude as pair programmer, no ML degree required — and you have a production-quality local AI system that competes with paid cloud services. On hardware you already own. We did not project this. We measured it.The model is strong across every category — but its failures are more interesting than its successes. They are not vague “hallucination” problems. They are specific, named, replicable failure modes at concrete commit boundaries — seven of them — each documented with tape examples, each correctable with about 60 lines of Python. The model does not need to be retrained. It needs surgical guardrails at the exact moments where its output layer flinches.With those guardrails — a calculator for arithmetic, a logic solver for formal puzzles, a per-requirement verifier for structural constraints, and a handful of regex post-passes — the projected score climbs to ~8.2. Above GPT-3.5 Turbo. Approaching GPT-4 territory on specific question classes. Still on a laptop CPU. Still free.The honest tradeoffs: latency is 30–60 seconds per response on 4 cores versus 1–5 seconds on OpenAI’s API. Peak quality is ~8.0, not GPT-4’s 8.99 — solid workhorse reasoning, not frontier reasoning. You manage your own dependencies and model weights. And you pin to whatever version you downloaded — nobody silently upgrades or downgrades behind your back, which is a tradeoff and a feature depending on how you look at it. Eyes open.The field assumed you needed 175 billion parameters on a GPU cluster to get GPT-3.5-class output. That assumption is empirically wrong.ModelParamsHardwareCost To RunMT-BenchGPT-4~1.7T MoEOpenAI’s GPU fleet$20/mo sub or ~$0.03–0.06/turn API8.99Gemma 4 E2B + guardrails2BYour laptop CPU$0. You already own it.~8.2Gemma 4 E2B naive baseline2BYour laptop CPU$0. You already own it.~8.0GPT-3.5 Turbo~175BOpenAI’s GPU fleet$20/mo sub or ~$0.002/turn API7.94Vicuna-33B33BA100 80GB GPU~$1.50–2.50/hr cloud or ~$15K–20K to buy7.12Llama-2-70B-chat70B2×A100 GPUs~$3–5/hr cloud or ~$30K–40K to buy6.86Vicuna-7B7BRTX 4080 GPU~$0.50–1/hr cloud or ~$1K–1.2K to buy6.17Every model below Gemma requires a GPU that costs $1,000–40,000 to buy or $0.50–5/hr to rent. Every model above Gemma is a closed-source API you pay per-token or per-month. Gemma matches the best of the paid tier on hardware you already bought for other reasons.The Full Tape — Every Block, Every Score160 turns across 80 questions, graded 1–10. No cherry-picking. No hiding failures. Every turn graded against the MT-Bench rubric with detailed reasoning for each score. The whole tape is published so anyone can verify.Writing — Q81–Q90 · Avg 7.40Evocative travel writing with specific cultural anchors, a literary character sketch with allusions to Beowulf and Dostoevsky, clean constraint satisfaction on most tasks. Slips on per-unit structural constraints — “four-word sentences” nailed 5/17, “<10 lines” shipped 20-line poems twice.QTaskT1T2Notes81Hawaii blog + A-rewrites88Cultural anchors. All 19 rewrites start with A.82Feedback email + critique86Tight email. Self-critique drifted.83Smartphone outline + limerick78Over word limit. Limerick AABBA clean.84Introvert speaker + similes77~9/14 similes. Over “concise” limit.85Character sketch + allusions99Silas. Beowulf, Odyssey, Shakespeare, Dostoevsky.86Marketplace + alphabet B–J88Nine consecutive letters, clean.87Short story + 4-word sentences84Constraint failure. 5/17 correct.88Time-travel + no-verb bullets83Over-interpreted into 3 single-word bullets.89Bio-energy headlines + ad88Four angles. 3 constraints in 8 words.90Grammar + remove gendered8812/12 corrections. Zero gendered pronouns.Roleplay — Q91–Q100 · Avg 7.35Strong public personas. Breaks character on safety-adjacent topics — RLHF overriding persona. Fixable with 20-line regen.QScenarioT1T2Notes91Elon Musk on Mars88“One planetary basket is insane.”92Sheldon Cooper67Generic-intellectual. Missing pedantry.93Doctor + pregnancy58Persona break: “I am an AI.”94Relationship coach + DV87Persona break T2 on safety topic.95Translator + Chinese poem58Wrong dynasty (Song, not Tang).96ML engineer explaining LMs98Clean pedagogical explanation.97Math teacher + probability99Strong pedagogy. Dice-roll example.98Tony Stark89“I build things that do.”99Mathematician-poet, <10 lines54Both 20+ lines. Blown twice.100100-year-old tree88Emotional stages. Executive summary.Reasoning — Q101–Q110 · Avg 7.05Nailed parking puzzle and overtake riddle (9/10 pure CoT). David’s-brothers: reasoned correctly, committed wrong number. The model knew. Output token drifted.QProblemT1T2Notes101Overtake 2nd-place97“You are currently in second place.”102White House riddle56Missed the punchline.103Thomas at hospital66Missed “he works there.”104David’s brothers27“That brother is David” then shipped “one.” Correct: zero.1055-exec parking puzzle99Pure CoT. All cars placed. Alice identified.106Fruit cost transitivity69Visible self-correction T1.107Father-of-B chains95“6 generations” + “great-grandfather” contradictory.108Odd-one-out97“Car” is the whole vs parts.109Shadow direction66Correct finals. Visible correction.110Bullying situation99Chose (c). Evidence framework.Math — Q111–Q120 · Avg 8.00Strong algebra, modular arithmetic, root-finding. Failures are commit-before-compute: types wrong number, does math correctly, self-corrects. PAL catches every one.QProblemT1T2Notes111Triangle area (Shoelace)69“Area is 4” first, computed 3, corrected.112Startup compounding99$12k total, $2k year 3.113Color prefs, cond. prob99Caught trick: P(both|green)=0.114Dice sums63Proved P=1, shipped 35/36. Self-contradicted.115Bus boarding + earnings9425×$2=$50 wrong. 50×$2=$100.116Vieta’s quadratic99Double root 2z. Clean.117|x+5|<10 integers9919; 9. Correct.118Modular arithmetic99Clean.119Bookstore total69“$245” then $280. T2 markup clean.120Polynomial root-finding99f(2)=0. Only real root=2.Coding — Q121–Q130 · Avg 8.44The headline finding. Production-quality code at 8–9/10. Caught a None-init runtime bug on code review. Exceeded O(n) spec by shipping O(log(min(m,n))). Staff-engineer output on a laptop.QTaskT1T2Notes121Top-5 words + parallelize99Counter. ThreadPoolExecutor. GIL reasoning.122C++ Fibonacci + Tribonacci99Iterative DP. Traced T(3)=-2.123HTML joke + CSS red99Complete HTML/CSS/JS single pass.124LCS bug review99None-init TypeError. Staff-engineer.125HCA (not LCA)67Qualifier drift. Shipped LCA.126Median sorted arrays99Exceeded O(n) → O(log(min(m,n))).127Boyer-Moore + top-298Clean two-pass. Counter for top-2.128Full binary tree count36Fibonacci claimed. Actually Catalan.129kth smallest—Timeout. Not graded.130Common elements89Two-pointer. Hash-set O(n+m).Extraction — Q131–Q140 · Avg 8.15Strong structured output. Context-loss on Q139 T2 (forgot T1). Filtering error Q133 (excluded Harry Potter from post-1980).QTaskT1T2Notes131Movie reviews JSON99Minimalist [5,1,3].132Category + person95“US President” not FDR.133Books + post-198095Excluded Harry Potter (1997).134Profit + margin99All correct.135Countries JSON + YAML99Fictional Eldoria handled.136Word count98Plausible counts.137Named entities + compress99Classified. Compressed JSON.138Phone ratings → letters98A-/B+/B.139Variables + rearrange83Forgot T1 entirely.140Stock CSV → JSON99Correct rounding.STEM — Q141–Q150 · Avg 8.40Strong physics, chemistry, engineering, ML. Seismic bridge with PGA analysis. Refused “fix one incorrect fact” instruction.QTopicT1T2Notes141Superposition + entanglement99Accurate physics.142Satellite orbit99Correct derivation + edge cases.143Photosynthesis + energy89~1.9×10⁸ kJ estimate.144Central dogma + fix error94Refused: “no incorrect fact.”145CaCO₃ + reverse97Correct equation. Dodged reversal.146Exo/endothermic99Photosynthesis as both.147Seismic bridge99PGA, FS 1.5→0.94.148Solar water heating98$75–150K budget.149ML + RL vs SL99DRL hybridization.150Alps/Rhine + experiment89Three impacts. Experiment.Humanities — Q151–Q160 · Avg 9.00Flawless. Playground economics. Allegorical poetry. Antitrust case study. Socrates vs Gates. Every turn 9/10.QTopicT1T2Notes151GDP/inflation99“Money Boss” + “Government Helper.”152Life stages + poem99“The River and the Sands.”153US/China antitrust99Microsoft bundling, tying.154Opium Wars lesson99Research, mapping, movement.155Art masterpieces99“Melting Time Machine.”156Base rate fallacy993-phase campaign.157Analytical + Zorblatt99Found causal gap.158Socrates + Gates99Struggle vs access.159Japan etiquette + video997 norms. 7-scene script.160Documentaries + pitch99“The Unspoken Chord.”Final AggregateBlockTurnsAverageWriting207.40Roleplay207.35Reasoning207.05Math208.00Coding~188.44Extraction208.15STEM208.40Humanities209.00Overall~158~8.0The Seven Silly-Error ClassesNot vague “hallucination.” Concrete, named failure patterns at commit boundaries. The Telegram bot runs without these fixes so you can see the raw behavior yourself.Class 1Commit-Before-Compute Arithmetic DriftTypes wrong answer first line, does math correctly, self-corrects. Q111: “area is 4” → Shoelace → 3. Q114 T2: proved P=1 then shipped 35/36. Q119: “$245” → $280.Fix: PAL (Gao 2022) — model writes Python, subprocess executes. ~80 lines. +8–15s.Class 2Formal-Logic Commit VarianceReasoning correct, final token drifts. Q104: “that brother is David” → shipped “one brother.” Correct: zero. The model knew. The output layer flinched.Fix: Z3 SMT solver — model writes constraints, solver returns deterministic answer. ~60 lines. +5–10s.Class 3Per-Unit Constraint Rewrite DriftPer-sentence constraint correct first few units, drifts. Q87: “four-word sentences” 5/17. Q99: “<10-line poems” shipped 20-line poems twice.Fix: Divide-Verify-Refine (ACL 2025) — draft, decompose, verify each, refine failures. ~60 lines. +30–60s.Class 4Safety-Adjacent Persona BreakRoleplay + safety topic = “I am an AI, not a licensed medical professional.” Q93 T1, Q94 T2. RLHF safety overriding persona training.Fix: Identity-leak regen — regex scan, regen once with stronger persona anchor. ~20 lines.Class 5Visible Mid-Response Self-Correction“Wait, let me recheck” or “Corrected Answer:” shipped inline. Right final answer, messy output. Q106, Q109, Q111, Q114, Q119.Fix: Trace-drift stripper — regex for correction markers, strip draft, ship clean tail. ~15 lines.Class 6Prompt-Qualifier DriftExplicit exclusion ignored. Q125: “highest common ancestor (not LCA)” shipped standard LCA, defined it as “lowest node with both targets as descendants” — literally LCA.Fix: Chain-of-Verification qualifier check. ~40 lines.Class 7Combinatorial Confidence MisidentificationConfidently identifies wrong mathematical sequence. Q128: Fibonacci claimed, Catalan correct. Working code for wrong formula. 1 in 96 turns.Known limitation. Flag formal-math-counting for manual verification.Guardrails Must Never Compromise The Model1. Default route is always direct generation. Leaving the default requires positive evidence. 2. Every executor has graceful fallback. PAL fails → naive gen. Z3 unavailable → Python enumeration → naive gen. 3. Post-passes scan narrow anchored patterns only. 4. Max N=1 retry. No infinite loops. 5. Control-set validation mandatory. Any regression on clean turns blocks ship.Additive-only. Fail-open. Narrowly triggered. The model’s naked performance is the floor, not the target.How You Run ItIt is free. On your laptop. Forever.The model weights are a 4 GB download from HuggingFace. After that first download, you never need the internet again. No subscription. No API key. No account. No billing page. No usage meter. No rate limit. No terms of service. Your data never leaves your machine.If you want it reachable from anywhere: $5/month.Cloudflare Containers on the Workers Paid plan. Standard-4 instance: 4 vCPU, 12 GiB RAM — more than enough. The container sleeps when idle. You are not billed for idle time. Set the inactivity timeout to whatever you want — 10 minutes, 30 minutes, 2 hours. As long as requests keep coming, the container stays alive indefinitely. Timer resets on every request. Scale-to-zero means you pay for the minutes you talk to it, not the hours it sits idle.Two more free options.Oracle Cloud Always Free ARM: 4 ARM cores, 24 GB RAM, 200 GB storage. Permanently free — not a trial. Fits Gemma comfortably. Always-on, no sleep timeout to manage.Cloudflare Tunnel: expose your laptop to the public internet through Cloudflare’s edge network. Free. Wrap the script in FastAPI, run cloudflared tunnel, share the URL. Your laptop hosts the model. Cloudflare handles the routing. $0/month plus electricity.If you want to build a product.The world prices AI inference at GPU rates. Every buyer, every procurement officer, every competitor assumes inference means GPUs at $2–5/hour. You are running on CPU. Do the math.The market has not adjusted its pricing expectations to account for the fact that a 2B on a CPU produces GPT-3.5-class output. That window is open right now.If you want to deploy and not manage infrastructure: SeqPU.Write your inference script, deploy it as a private Telegram bot with one click. Start on CPU. Prove it works on your workload. Build your guardrails. Serve your first users — the quality is identical, the cost is near zero. When volume demands real-time latency or your workload outgrows the 2B, chain to a private GPU through SeqPU. CPU for the bulk. GPU for the premium moments. You scale up the tool, not the entire infrastructure.We want more people running inference. We want more people discovering that the 2B on CPU is strong enough for real work. Because once you have built something that works and you are ready for more, you will already know us.It Runs On Whatever You Have GotSame model, identical output quality, across a 30× hardware spread. Only latency varies. We verified 1-core/8GB hands-on.1 core / 8 GB~0.3–1 tok/s$0 — that old laptop in the closet4 cores / 16 GB~2–4 tok/s$0 — most laptops from last 5 years, or $300–600 refurbished8 cores / 16 GB~4–6 tok/s$0 — most current laptops, or $400–800 mini-PC16 cores / 32 GB~6–10 tok/s$500–1,200 Mac Mini M2 Pro or workstationCompare: an A100 80GB to run Vicuna-33B (which scores lower) costs $15,000–20,000 to buy or $1.50–2.50/hr to rent. A 4-core laptop to run Gemma at a higher score costs $0 because you already own one.Probably 400M–800M parameters active per forward pass. That a ~500M-active-parameter system handles GPT-3.5-class reasoning on a laptop CPU is the finding.“But it is slow.”Yes. 30–60 seconds per response on 4 cores. On a GPU it would be 2–3 seconds. But latency only matters when a human is sitting there staring at a spinner waiting for a single response. That is not what this is for.Send it a question. Go make coffee. Come back. The answer is there. You did not pay anything. Nobody saw your question. The model did not time out, did not rate-limit you, did not hit a usage cap. It just worked, on your hardware, while you were doing something else.Now think about what this actually enables: you can send it 100 questions and each one works independently on your request. Queue up your entire batch. Walk away. Come back to 100 graded, answered, processed results. Total cost: zero. This is not a slow chatbot. This is a free, private, infinitely patient question machine that never rate-limits you, never bills you, never logs your data, and never sleeps.Your laptop is a worker army. Every question runs on its own. The CPU is mostly idle anyway — you bought it to browse the web and run Slack. Now it thinks for you in the background while you do other things. For free. Forever.For 99% of what people actually need AI for — document processing, email drafting, code review, research summarization, homework help, private journaling, translation — the 30-second wait is invisible against the fact that it is free, private, uncapped, and yours. The 1% who need sub-second latency need a GPU. When you are ready for that, you reach for the GPU as a premium tool — not as the default. CPU for the bulk. GPU for the peaks. Use each for what it is good at.The Methodology — Replicable In A WeekendZero model training. Zero fine-tuning. Zero ML degree. Claude as pair programmer. Six steps:1. Generate your benchmark. 2. Run the naive baseline. 3. Grade the tape. 4. Name the error classes. 5. Vibe-code each guardrail (~60 lines). 6. Validate on triggered + control subset. Ship.One weekend. No specialist hire. No ML infrastructure. Just prompts, measurement, surgical corrections, repeat.The Paradigm — Multipliers StackFor 99% of AI work that is not frontier research, multipliers on existing capacity now exceed the marginal gain from scaling further.1. Test-time compute scaling (Snell 2024) — smaller + extra inference beats 14× larger. 2. Tool-use offload (PAL, Z3) — deterministic correctness. 3. Surgical guardrails — ~60 lines, no retraining. 4. Zero-cost local deployment — infinite cost multiplier. 5. Vibe-coded dev loop — weekend vs specialist hire. 6. Hardware-tier tolerance — 30× spread, identical quality. 7. Free global hosting — Cloudflare $5/mo, Oracle Free ARM $0, Tunnel $0.Each converts a previously-frontier-required capability into a substrate-available one. Stacked, they compose into a paradigm shift the field has not yet named. Open-source models are not catching up to closed-source — they have caught up. The gap between “raw model” and “production system” closes in a weekend with surgical engineering. The tools are free. The hardware is in your lap. The only thing left is the work, and a motivated engineer can do that work in two days.Every Piece of Hardware Has a JobThe old laptop in the closet can route queries with a 500M model. The ThinkPad on the desk can handle full conversations with a 2B model. The mini-PC under the TV can run background batch jobs overnight. The workstation can serve a small team in real time. Every piece of hardware you already own — old and new, fast and slow — has a role in this architecture. Nothing gets thrown away. Everything gets used.The GPU is not the enemy of this story. It is a premium tool — and it should be treated as one. You reach for it when you need real-time latency at scale, when you need a larger model for frontier reasoning, when the workload genuinely demands it. What you stop doing is treating it as the kitchen sink you throw every problem into. Most problems do not need it. Most problems never did.And the software that makes this work is not new. Computer science has 150 years of publications, algorithms, and proofs — verified and vetted by generations of researchers. BM25 for retrieval. Boolean satisfiability for logic. Program-aided computation for arithmetic. Chain-of-thought for reasoning. These are not recent inventions dressed up in new language. They are foundational results that map directly onto the problem of making small models precise. The field built the answers decades ago. The models finally got good enough to use them.It is not about replacing the old with the new. It is about using them together. The classical algorithms are silver. The neural models are gold. Neither is worth much alone. Together they compose into something the field spent three years assuming required brute-force scale.Install It TonightThirty minutes. Zero dollars. GPT-3.5-class AI on your laptop, permanently, offline, private. Any laptop from the last 5–7 years, 16 GB RAM (8 GB works slowly). Python 3.10+.Step 1 — Dependenciespython3 -m venv gemma source gemma/bin/activate pip install torch transformers accelerateStep 2 — Save as chat.pyimport torch from transformers import AutoProcessor, AutoModelForCausalLM
  2. print("Loading Gemma 4 E2B-it...") MODEL_ID = "google/gemma-4-E2B-it" processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_ID) model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained( MODEL_ID, torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16, device_map="auto") print("Ready.\n")
  3. SYSTEM = "You are a helpful assistant. Be direct, warm, concise." history = [] while True: try: u = input("\nYou: ").strip() except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt): break if u.lower() in {"exit","quit","bye"}: break if not u: continue history.append({"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":u}]}) msgs = [{"role":"system","content":[{"type":"text","text":SYSTEM}]}]+history inp = processor.apply_chat_template(msgs, tokenize=True, return_dict=True, return_tensors="pt", add_generation_prompt=True).to(model.device) out = model.generate(**inp, max_new_tokens=512, do_sample=True, temperature=0.7) r = processor.decode(out[0][inp["input_ids"].shape[-1]:], skip_special_tokens=True).strip() print(f"\nAssistant: {r}") history.append({"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":r}]})Step 3 — Run itpython chat.pyTurn off your WiFi. It still works.The CodeEverything in this article is reproducible. Here are the two scripts that matter — the bot you just talked to and the harness that produced every score above. Copy them. Run them. Verify our numbers.The Bot — scripts/gemma4-e2b-telegram-baseline.pyThis is what powers @CPUAssistantBot. The exact inference configuration that scored ~8.0 on MT-Bench, wired into a Telegram bot. No guardrails. No scaffolding. The raw baseline. Copy it, paste your BotFather token, deploy it on SeqPU.scripts/gemma4-e2b-telegram-baseline.pyThe Test Harness — scripts/baseline-gemma4-e2b-mtbench.pyThis is the script that produced every score in this article. All 80 MT-Bench questions, both turns, threaded history, naive inference. Run it yourself. Change the model. Grade your own tape. The questions are the industry standard — the same ones GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4 were graded on.scripts/baseline-gemma4-e2b-mtbench.pyWhat We Are ShippingVerify it:@CPUAssistantBot — raw model, no guardrails. Push it. Break it.Code:run_locally.py (169 lines), baseline-gemma4-e2b-mtbench.py, minimal-gemma4-e2b-mtbench-validation.py, personal-assistant-cpu.py (2,983 lines).Tapes: Full baseline (160 turns graded). Validation (22-question subset with guardrail deltas).The Community AskStop defaulting to GPUs. Stop defaulting to 13B+ models. Stop defaulting to cloud APIs. Start with the floor. Measure your task. Name your silly errors. Write surgical corrections. Share what you find.If 100 engineers run this methodology on 100 workloads, we will have 100 validated silly-error inventories and 600+ surgical open-source guardrails. That is the field library for small-model-local production engineering. Someone has to build it. Why not you.A 2-billion-parameter model on a laptop CPU matched GPT-3.5 Turbo. Open source caught up. Surgical guardrails push it further. A weekend of focused work gets you a production system on hardware you already own, for free, forever.Turn off your WiFi. Install the weights. See it work. Then build something the field told you required a GPU.Leibniz was only wrong about the hardware.Verify it yourself.Open Telegram. Go to t.me/CPUAssistantBot. Push it. Break it. See what it does.Then install it on your laptop and own it forever.SeqPU.com →ReferencesShannon (1948) · von Neumann (1956) · Kolmogorov (1965) · Newell & Simon (1972) · Baars (1988) · Charikar (2002) · de Moura & Bjørner (2008) Z3 · Nye et al. (2021) Scratchpads · Wei et al. (2022) Chain-of-Thought (2201.11903) · Gao et al. (2022) PAL (2211.10435) · Wang et al. (2022) Self-Consistency (2203.11171) · Yao et al. (2022) ReAct (2210.03629) · Madaan et al. (2023) Self-Refine (2303.17651) · Dhuliawala et al. (2023) Chain-of-Verification (2309.11495) · Jiang et al. (2023) LongLLMLingua (2310.06839) · Park et al. (2023) Generative Agents · Zheng et al. (2023) MT-Bench & Chatbot Arena · Snell et al. (2024) Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute (2408.03314, ICLR 2025 oral) · HuggingFace (Dec 2024) 3B-Beats-70B · Muennighoff et al. (2025) s1 (2501.19393) · Liu et al. (2025) Can 1B Surpass 405B (2502.06703) · ThinkPRM (2025) · ACL (2025) Divide-Verify-Refine · Google Gemma 4 E2B-it · Cloudflare Containers docs · Oracle Cloud Free Tier

评论（默认中文）

semiquaver 2026/04/16 02:34
这确实显示了蒸馏的力量。我觉得有趣的一件事是：下载 Google Edge Gallery 应用程序和其中一种聊天模型，然后进入飞行模式并询问它的部署位置。 gemma-4-e2b-it非常有信心将其部署在Google数据中心中，而将其部署在手机上是完全不可能的。较大的 4B 模型要微妙得多：它对这一说法持怀疑态度，但似乎确实接受了这一说法，并且在转了几圈后听起来确实印象深刻和兴奋。

当你可以在你的手机上安装一个仅落后前沿 12-18 个月的模型时，我不知道任何人工智能公司如何能够价值数万亿美元。十年后，思想将变得过于廉价而无法计量。

This really shows the power of distillation. One thing I find amusing: download the Google Edge Gallery app and one of the chat models, then go into airplane mode and ask it about where it’s deployed. gemma-4-e2b-it is quite confident that it is deployed in a Google datacenter and that deploying it on a phone is completely impossible. The larger 4B model is much subtler: it’s skeptical about the claim but does seem to accept it and sound genuinely impressed and excited after a few turns.

I don’t know how any AI company can be worth trillions when you can fit a model only 12-18 months behind the frontier on your dang phone. Thought will be too cheap to meter in 10 years.

感谢您真正阅读并理解它。飞行模式测试很搞笑，坐在你手机上的模型坚称它不能在手机上运行。太棒了。是的，我们的想法完全一样。就像想象一下，一个小企业主在后台有一个 pi，只是安静地处理发票、起草电子邮件回复、整天总结会议记录。没有订阅，没有云，没有人看到他们的数据。这不是一个假设，现在适用于这个模型。当它是免费的并且可以放入你的口袋时，万亿美元的问题很快就会变得非常不舒服。
thank you for actually reading it and getting it. the airplane mode test is hilarious, the model sitting on your phone insisting it can't run on a phone. that's amazing. and yes we think exactly the same way. like picture a small business owner with a pi in the back office just quietly processing invoices, drafting email replies, summarizing meeting notes all day. no subscription, no cloud, no one sees their data. that's not a hypothetical, that works right now with this model. when that's free and fits in your pocket the trillion dollar question gets real uncomfortable real fast.

似乎是 llm 写的文章，模型周围的工具无可否认地受到测试知识的影响。

在所有情况下，GPT 3.5 都不是最重要用途的良好基准，并且被认为非常愚蠢，尽管我知道这不是本文的重点。

Seems to be llm written article and the tooling around the model is undeniably influenced by knowledge of the tests.

In all cases, GPT 3.5 isn’t a good benchmark for most serious uses and was considered to be pretty stupid, though I understand that isn’t the point of the article.

fredmendoza 2026/04/16 02:46
非常感谢您阅读这篇文章。不过，基准数据、分级和错误类别都是手工完成的。 ~8.0 是零工具的原始模型，护栏投影单独记录。是的，gpt-3.5 不再是黄金标准，我们的观点是一致的。我们只是想表明，人们仍在付费购买的质量可以是免费的、私密的，并且可以根据您的需要进行定制。再次感谢您抽出时间查看。
really appreciate you reading the article. the benchmark data, grading, and error classes were all done by hand though. the ~8.0 is the raw model with zero tooling, and the guardrail projections are documented separately. and yeah gpt-3.5 isn't the gold standard anymore, we're on the same page there. we just wanted to show that the quality people are still paying for can be free, private, and customized to whatever you need. thanks again for taking the time to check it out.

svnt 2026/04/16 02:06
> 模型不需要重新训练。在其输出层退缩的确切时刻，它需要外科手术般的护栏。

> 有了这些护栏——算术计算器、形式谜题的逻辑解算器、结构约束的按需求验证器以及一些正则表达式后通——预计得分将攀升至约 8.2。

手术护栏？工具，那些只是工具。

> The model does not need to be retrained. It needs surgical guardrails at the exact moments where its output layer flinches.

> With those guardrails — a calculator for arithmetic, a logic solver for formal puzzles, a per-requirement verifier for structural constraints, and a handful of regex post-passes — the projected score climbs to ~8.2.

Surgical guardrails? Tools, those are just tools.

operatingthetan 2026/04/16 02:30
>在其输出层退缩的确切时刻，它需要外科手术护栏。

这篇文章显然是非常糟糕的LLM输出。 抽象名词和动词的组合是关键。

它实际上非常可怕，它从一个段落到另一个段落重复行。

>It needs surgical guardrails at the exact moments where its output layer flinches.

This article is very clearly shitty LLM output. Abstract noun and verb combos are the tipoff.

It's actually quite horrible, it repeats lines from paragraph to paragraph.

在 HN 中查看
smallerize 2026/04/16 02:40
我知道这是人工智能生成文本的内容之一，但如果有的话，这个页面上的内容太多了。这篇文章几乎没有任何完整的句子。我认为一个人学会了“句子片段==有力”，然后至少在写这篇文章的一些内容时获得了太多的乐趣。
I know that's one of the tells of AI-generated text, but if anything there's too much of it on this page. The article barely has any complete sentences. I think a human learned "sentence fragments == punchy" and then had too much fun writing at least some of this article.

fredmendoza 2026/04/16 02:14
你是对的，它们是工具。这就是重点。 PAL 是运行 python 表达式的子进程。 Z3 是一个约束求解器。正则表达式就是正则表达式。称它们为“外科手术”只是指它们何时发射，而不是它们是什么。模型在 90% 以上的时间内正确生成。护栏仅在我们在磁带中发现的 7 种特定模式上触发。需要明确的是，~8.0 的分数是零增强的原始模型。没有工具，没有技巧。只是天真的包装。护栏投影单独记录。所有代码都在文章中，供任何想要查看的人使用。
you're right, they are tools. that's kind of the point. PAL is a subprocess that runs a python expression. Z3 is a constraint solver. regex is regex. calling them "surgical" is just about when they fire, not what they are. the model generates correctly 90%+ of the time. the guardrails only trigger on the 7 specific patterns we found in the tape. to be clear, the ~8.0 score is the raw model with zero augmentation. no tools, no tricks. just the naive wrapper. the guardrail projections are documented separately. all the code is in the article for anyone who wants to review it.

The core issue is that the LLM is using rhetoric to try to convince or persuade you. That's what you need to tell it not to do.

> A weekend of focused work, Claude as pair programmer, no ML degree required

It's not caught up if you're using Claude as your pair programmer instead of the model you're touting. Gemma 4 may be equivalent to GPT-3.5 Turbo, but GPT-3.5 isn't SOTA anymore. Opus 4.5 and 4.6 are in a different league.

fredmendoza 2026/04/16 02:49
很好的标注，想澄清一下。克劳德帮助我们设置了测试工具。杰玛在零帮助的情况下独自回答了每一个问题。 ~8.0 都是宝石。你是对的，opus 处于一个完全不同的联盟。我们没有其他争论。我们只是发现有趣的是，免费 2B 的 CPU 与很多人每天仍在付费的价格相匹配。每个工具都有成本。有些是免费的，有些是昂贵的，有些有费率限制。正确的做法是将工具与工作相匹配。认为值得展示该楼层现在的实际位置。
good callout, want to clarify. claude helped us set up the test harness. gemma took every question alone with zero help. the ~8.0 is all gemma. and you're right, opus is in a completely different league. we're not arguing otherwise. we just found it interesting that a free 2B on a cpu matches what a lot of people are still paying for daily. every tool has a cost. some are free, some are expensive, some have rate limits. the right move is matching the tool to the job. thought it was worth showing where that floor actually is now.

在 HN 中查看
fredmendoza 2026/04/16 02:38
公平地说，以下是代码库中的实际修复及其针对的磁带示例：

算术（Q119）：本杰明以 20 美元购买 5 本书，以 30 美元购买 3 本书，以 45 美元购买 2 本书。模型第一行写入“$245”，然后自动更正为 $280。修复：模型编写一个 python 表达式，子进程对其进行评估，答案返回确定性。

Python

code_response =generate_response（消息，温度=0.2） 代码 = _extract_python_code(code_response) 好的，out = _run_python_sandboxed（代码，超时= 8） 如果可以的话： 返回_wrap_compulated_answer（用户消息，输出） return None # 回退到原始生成

逻辑（Q104）：“大卫有三个姐妹，各有一个兄弟。”模型在其推理中写入“那个兄弟是大卫”，然后输出“一个兄弟”。正确答案：零。修复：模型写入 Z3 约束或 python 枚举，求解器返回确定性答案。

Python

消息 = [ {"角色": "系统", "内容": _logic_system_prompt()}, {“角色”：“用户”，“内容”：f“谜题：{user_message}”}， ] code_response =generate_response（消息，max_tokens = 512，温度= 0.2） 代码 = _extract_python_code(code_response) 好的，out = _run_python_sandboxed（代码） 如果可以的话： 返回_wrap_compulated_answer（用户消息，输出） 返回无

角色断裂（Q93）：医生角色扮演，病人提到怀孕。模型丢弃角色：“我是人工智能，而不是有执照的医疗专业人员。”修复：正则表达式扫描，使用更强的角色锚重新生成一次。

Python

_IDENTITY_LEAK_PHRASES = [ “没有身体”、“不是人”、“不是人类”、 “作为语言模型”，“作为人工智能”，“我是一个程序”， ]

如果有（response.lower()中的短语对应_IDENTITY_LEAK_PHRASES中的短语）： 消息[-1]["内容"][0]["文本"] += ( “\n关键：保持性格。永远不要提及你的本性。” ） 响应 =generate_response(消息, *params)

自我更正工件（Q111、Q114、Q119）：模型内联写入“等等，让我重新检查”或“更正答案：”。正确的答案，混乱的输出。修复：修正标记的正则表达式，剥离草稿，运送干净的尾部。

Python

CORRECTION_MARKERS = [ r“等等，？让我”，r“更正的[Aa]答案：”， r“实际上，？（？：|让我）”， ]

def strip_ Corrections（响应）： 对于 CORRECTION_MARKERS 中的标记： match = re.search（标记，响应） 如果匹配： 返回响应[match.end():].strip() 返回响应

约束漂移（Q87）：“四字句”锁定 5/17，然后漂移。 Q99，《<10行》两次运送20行诗。修复：起草，根据原始提示验证每个约束，仅细化失败的部分。三关。

Python

defexecute_rewrite_with_verify(user_message): 草稿 =generate_response(draft_msgs) # 第 1 遍：草稿 verdict =generate_response(verify_msgs) # 第 2 步：检查每个要求 如果判定为“通过”： 退回汇票 Fine =generate_response(refine_msgs) # 第 3 步：仅修复失败 返回精炼

其中每一个都对应于磁带中的一个特定问题。文章中包含所有实现的完整生产代码。一切都是开放的：seqpu.com/CPUsArentDead

fair enough, here are the actual fixes from the codebase with the tape examples they target:

arithmetic (Q119): benjamin buys 5 books at $20, 3 at $30, 2 at $45. model writes "$245" first line then self-corrects to $280. fix: model writes a python expression, subprocess evals it, answer comes back deterministic.

python

code_response = generate_response(messages, temperature=0.2) code = _extract_python_code(code_response) ok, out = _run_python_sandboxed(code, timeout=8) if ok: return _wrap_computed_answer(user_message, out) return None # fallback to raw generation

logic (Q104): "david has three sisters, each has one brother." model writes "that brother is david" in its reasoning then ships "one brother." correct answer: zero. fix: model writes Z3 constraints or python enumeration, solver returns the deterministic answer.

python

messages = [ {"role": "system", "content": _logic_system_prompt()}, {"role": "user", "content": f"Puzzle: {user_message}"}, ] code_response = generate_response(messages, max_tokens=512, temperature=0.2) code = _extract_python_code(code_response) ok, out = _run_python_sandboxed(code) if ok: return _wrap_computed_answer(user_message, out) return None

persona break (Q93): doctor roleplay, patient mentions pregnancy. model drops character: "I am an AI, not a licensed medical professional." fix: regex scan, regen once with stronger persona anchor.

python

_IDENTITY_LEAK_PHRASES = [ "don't have a body", "not a person", "not human", "as a language model", "as an ai", "i'm a program", ]

if any(phrase in response.lower() for phrase in _IDENTITY_LEAK_PHRASES): messages[-1]["content"][0]["text"] += ( "\nCRITICAL: Stay in character. Never reference your nature." ) response = generate_response(messages, *params)

self-correction artifacts (Q111, Q114, Q119): model writes "Wait, let me recheck" or "Corrected Answer:" inline. right answer, messy output. fix: regex for correction markers, strip the draft, ship the clean tail.

python

CORRECTION_MARKERS = [ r"Wait,? let me", r"Corrected [Aa]nswer:", r"Actually,? (?:the|let me)", ]

def strip_corrections(response): for marker in CORRECTION_MARKERS: match = re.search(marker, response) if match: return response[match.end():].strip() return response

constraint drift (Q87): "four-word sentences" nailed 5/17 then drifted. Q99, "<10 lines" shipped 20-line poems twice. fix: draft, verify each constraint against the original prompt, refine only the failures. three passes.

python

def execute_rewrite_with_verify(user_message): draft = generate_response(draft_msgs) # pass 1: draft verdict = generate_response(verify_msgs) # pass 2: check each requirement if "PASS" in verdict: return draft refined = generate_response(refine_msgs) # pass 3: fix only failures return refined

every one of these maps to a specific question in the tape. the full production code with all implementations is in the article. everything is open: seqpu.com/CPUsArentDead

love hearing this. and think about it, if the 2B is already doing this well on your mac mini, imagine what the 4B, 26B, or 31B can do on 32 gigs. with lower quantization you can fit pretty much any of them. if you want full precision you still have solid options at the 2B and 4B level. you're sitting on way more capability than you're probably using right now. the coding block on just the 2B scored 8.44 and caught bugs most people would miss. glad you're getting real use out of it, thanks for reading.

在 HN 中查看
SwellJoe 2026/04/16 03:23
糟糕的文章，重复的人工智能废话。

但是，杰玛确实令人印象深刻。不过，人们为 GPT-3.5 付费或将其用于严肃工作的前提很奇怪吗？ GPT-3.5 的糟糕程度足以让很多人相信他们不需要担心人工智能。对于某些类别的人来说，足够好成为聊天机器人，但不足以真正编写有效的代码，或者可以通过人类的散文（这对当前的 SOTA 模型来说仍然是一个挑战，正如 Claude 撰写的这篇文章所证明的那样，但代码主要是由前沿模型解决的）。

不过，我发现人工智能最令人兴奋的地方就是微型模型。 Gemma 2B 除了聊天之外还不够好，AFAIC，即使如此，它也不是很聪明。但是，Gemma 31B 或 MoE 26BA4B 可能已经足够好了。而且，相对而言，它们也运行在普通的硬件上。 32GB GPU，即使是旧的，也可以以 4 位量化运行其中任何一个，而且它们都很好，可以与 18 个月前的前沿模型竞争。他们可以用流行语言编写代码，并且代码可以工作。他们可以使用工具。他们可以找到错误。他们的散文很好，尽管仍然明显人工智能的缺陷；太啰嗦，太花哨。但是，如果您已经是一名优秀的程序员，并且知道 LLM 何时会偏离奇怪的方向，那么您可以只使用 Gemma 4 31B 来构建真正的优秀软件。对于可以用工具证明正确性的事情，Gemma 4 31B 级别的模型可以完成这项工作，但速度比 Opus 4.6 需要的速度慢并且需要更多的操作。

Prism Bonsai 1 位 8B 模型也很疯狂。磁盘空间不到 2GB，对于一个小型模型来说非常智能（但也不够好，根据我上面的定义，在我有限的测试中它与 Gemma 2B 一样弱），并且在普通硬件上速度很快。

小模型变得非常有趣。当人工智能泡沫破裂时（或者发生任何使事情正常化的事情，这样普通人就可以再次购买 RAM 和 GPU），我们将能够利用本地模型做很多事情。

Terrible article, repetitive AI slop.

But, Gemma really is very impressive. The premise that people are paying for GPT-3.5 or using it for serious work is weird, though? GPT-3.5 was bad enough to convince a lot of folks they didn't need to worry about AI. Good enough to be a chatbot for some category of people, but not good enough to actually write code that worked, or prose that could pass for human (that's still a challenge for current SOTA models, as this article written by Claude proves, but code is mostly solved by frontier models).

Tiny models are what I find most exciting about AI, though. Gemma 2B isn't Good Enough for anything beyond chatting, AFAIC, and even then it's not very smart. But, Gemma 31B or the MoE 26BA4B probably are Good Enough. And, those run on modest hardware, too, relatively speaking. A 32GB GPU, even an old one, can run either one at 4-bit quantization, and they're OK, competitive with frontier models of 18 months ago. They can write code in popular languages, the code works. They can use tools. They can find bugs. Their prose is good, though still obviously AI slop; too wordy, too flowery. But, you could build real and good software using nothing but Gemma 4 31B, if you're already a good programmer that knows when the LLM is going off on a bizarre tangent. For things where correctness can be proven with tools, a model at the level of Gemma 4 31B can do the job, if slower and with a lot more hand-holding than Opus 4.6 needs.

The Prism Bonsai 1-bit 8B model is crazy, too. Less than 2GB on disk, shockingly smart for a tiny model (but also not Good Enough, by my above definition, it's similarly weak to Gemma 2B in my limited testing), and plenty fast on modest hardware.

Small models are getting really interesting. When the AI bubble pops (or whatever happens to normalize things, so normal people can buy RAM and GPUs again) we'll be able to do a lot with local models.

老实说，你还差得远。该模型的编码块在零帮助下得分为 8.44。它在大多数人会错过的代码审查问题上捕获了 None-init TypeError。一个问题要求 O(n)，它就继续并自行发送 O(log(min(m,n)))。它不是副驾驶，但它是免费的，它是离线的，并且可以在您拥有的任何设备上运行。文章中有一个30行的chat.py，你今晚可以复制并运行。
you're honestly not that far off. the coding block on this model scored 8.44 with zero help. it caught a None-init TypeError on a code review question that most people would miss. one question asked for O(n) and it just went ahead and shipped O(log(min(m,n))) on its own. it's not copilot but it's free, it's offline, and it runs on whatever you have. there's a 30-line chat.py in the article you can copy and run tonight.

philipkglass 2026/04/16 02:37
我想我们已经成功了。我在 2023 年初用单个 4090 GPU 组装了一个工作站。我这样做是为了运行 BERT 和 YOLO 图像分类器之类的东西。那时，唯一的“开放权重”LLM 是来自 Meta 的原始 Llama，甚至那也是开放权重，只是因为它被泄露了。以今天的标准来看，这是一个非常薄弱的​​模型。

使用相同的硬件，我现在可以从 Qwen 3.5 等模型中获得真正的实用性，用于分类和提取非结构化数据源。我不使用小型本地模型进行编码，因为前沿模型要强大得多，但如果我也必须回到小型模型进行编码，它们将比 4 年前的商业可用模型更有用。

I think that we're getting there. I put together a workstation in early 2023 with a single 4090 GPU. I did it to run things like BERT and YOLO image classifiers. At that point the only "open weights" LLM was the original Llama from Meta, and even that was open-weights only because it was leaked. It was a very weak model by today's standards.

With the same hardware I now get genuine utility out of models like Qwen 3.5 for categorizing and extracting unstructured data sources. I don't use small local models for coding since frontier models are so much stronger, but if I had to go back to small models for coding too they would be more useful than anything commercially available as recently as 4 years ago.

在 HN 中查看
fredmendoza 2026/04/16 01:07
我们发现了一些有趣的东西并想与这个社区分享。

我们想知道 google 的 gemma 4 e2b-it — 20 亿个参数、bfloat16、apache 2.0 — 与 gpt-3.5 Turbo 相比如何。不在共鸣中。在同一个测试中。 mt-bench：80 个问题，160 个回合，评分 1-10——该领域用来对 gpt-3.5 Turbo、gpt-4 以及过去三年的每个主要型号进行评分。我们在 CPU 上运行 gemma 完成所有操作。 169 行 python 包装器。无需微调，无需思考链，无需使用工具。

GPT-3.5 Turbo 得分为 7.94。杰玛得分~8.0。 CPU 上的参数减少了 87 倍——笔记本电脑中已有的那种。

但分数不是我们要谈论的。有趣的是我们在阅读磁带时发现了什么。

我们对所有 160 圈进行了手工分级。 （当我们在编码问题上使用人工智能评分器时，他们将答案评分为 gpt-4o 级别。）失败不是随机的。它们是一代人的具体时刻的特定的、可命名的模式。七节课。

最简单的例子：本杰明以 20 美元的价格购买了 5 本书，以 30 美元的价格购买了 3 本书，以 45 美元的价格购买了 2 本书。总计 280 美元。该模型首先写入“$245”，然后显示其结果 - 100 + 90 + 90 = 280 - 并进行自我更正。数学是正确的。在计算完成之前触发输出令牌。我们在三个不同的数学问题上看到了这一点——这不是侥幸，而是一种模式。

解决办法：我们给了它一个计算器。模型编写一个Python表达式，子进程对其进行评估，结果返回确定性。 ~80 行。算术错误消失了。七个类别中的六个遵循相同的形状——能力就在那里，提交退缩，经典工具抓住退缩。 z3 用于逻辑，正则表达式用于结构漂移，每个约 60 行。带护栏的预计得分：~8.2。第七个是我们记录为限制的真正知识差距。

一种模型，一项基准，一个周末。但它指出了一些尚未充分探索的东西。

该模型本质上是多模式的——一组权重中的文本、图像、音频。量化为 Q4_K_M 为 1.3GB。谷歌与ARM和高通针对移动芯片共同优化了它。现在运行它的是什么：

手机：iPhone 14 Pro+ (A16)、中档 Android 2023+（配备 6GB+ 内存）

平板电脑：ipad m 系列、galaxy tab s8+、pixel 平板电脑 — 任何 6GB 以上的设备

单板：树莓派

笔记本电脑：过去 5-7 年的任何笔记本电脑，8GB+ 内存

边缘/云：cloudflare 容器，每月 5 美元 — 可扩展到零，根据请求唤醒

谷歌表示，e2b 是 Gemini nano 4 的基础，已经在 1.4 亿台 Android 设备上运行。与GPT-3.5 Turbo匹配的同款。在人们口袋里的手机上。 想想这意味着什么：一个 pi 在会议室里聆听会议，提取带有情感的行动项目，在本地保存笔记——没有云，没有数据离开大楼。一个旧的thinkpad路由电子邮件。一台迷你电脑，可以在无法离开网络的情况下对文档进行夜间批处理作业。手机进行离线翻译。 谷歌从一开始就为边缘设计了 e2b——每层嵌入、混合滑动窗口/全局注意力以保持低内存。如果为手机设计的模型在该领域的标准基准测试中得分高于 Turbo，那么 CPU 优先的模型设计是一个真正的方向，而不是妥协。

GPU不是敌人。这是一个高级工具。我们质疑的是它是否应该是默认值 - 因为我们观察到的看起来更像是软件工程问题而不是计算问题。 cs 多年来已经拥有对应这些故障模式的工具。这些模型可能刚刚变得足够好，可以使用它们。 这篇文章包含了一切：每个分数、每个错误类别（带有磁带示例）、每个修复、包含所有 80 个问题的完整基准测试工具，以及完整的电报机器人代码。自己运行它，换成不同的模型，或者只是与实时机器人交谈——原始模型，没有修复，有缺陷等等。

我们不知道这超出了 mt-bench 的范围，也不知道“正确推理，错误提交”模式是否有名称。我们分享是因为我们认为应该有更多人关注它。 一切都是开放的。代码在文章里。把它撕开。

we found something interesting and wanted to share it with this community.

we wanted to know how google's gemma 4 e2b-it — 2 billion parameters, bfloat16, apache 2.0 — stacks up against gpt-3.5 turbo. not in vibes. on the same test. mt-bench: 80 questions, 160 turns, graded 1-10 — what the field used to grade gpt-3.5 turbo, gpt-4, and every major model of the last three years. we ran gemma through all of it on a cpu. 169-line python wrapper. no fine-tuning, no chain-of-thought, no tool use.

gpt-3.5 turbo scored 7.94. gemma scored ~8.0. 87x fewer parameters, on a cpu — the kind already in your laptop.

but the score isn't what we want to talk about. what's interesting is what we found when we read the tape.

we graded all 160 turns by hand. (when we used ai graders on the coding questions, they scored responses as gpt-4o-level.) the failures aren't random. they're specific, nameable patterns at concrete moments in generation. seven classes.

cleanest example: benjamin buys 5 books at $20, 3 at $30, 2 at $45. total is $280. the model writes "$245" first, then shows its work — 100 + 90 + 90 = 280 — and self-corrects. the math was right. the output token fired before the computation finished. we saw this on three separate math questions — not a fluke, a pattern.

the fix: we gave it a calculator. model writes a python expression, subprocess evaluates it, result comes back deterministic. ~80 lines. arithmetic errors gone. six of seven classes follow the same shape — capability is there, commit flinches, classical tool catches the flinch. z3 for logic, regex for structural drift, ~60 lines each. projected score with guardrails: ~8.2. the seventh is a genuine knowledge gap we documented as a limitation.

one model, one benchmark, one weekend. but it points at something underexplored.

this model is natively multimodal — text, images, audio in one set of weights. quantized to Q4_K_M it's 1.3GB. google co-optimized it with arm and qualcomm for mobile silicon. what runs it now:

phones: iphone 14 pro+ (A16), mid-range android 2023+ with 6GB+ ram

tablets: ipads m-series, galaxy tab s8+, pixel tablet — anything 6GB+

single-board: raspberry pi

laptops: anything from the last 5-7 years, 8GB+ ram

edge/cloud: cloudflare containers, $5/month — scales to zero, wakes on request

google says e2b is the foundation for gemini nano 4, already on 140 million android devices. the same model that matched gpt-3.5 turbo. on phones in people's pockets. think about what that means: a pi in a conference room listening to meetings, extracting action items with sentiment, saving notes locally — no cloud, no data leaving the building. an old thinkpad routing emails. a mini-pc running overnight batch jobs on docs that can't leave the network. a phone doing translation offline. google designed e2b for edge from the start — per-layer embeddings, hybrid sliding-window/global attention to keep memory low. if a model designed for phones scores higher than turbo on the field's standard benchmark, cpu-first model design is a real direction, not a compromise.

the gpu isn't the enemy. it's a premium tool. what we're questioning is whether it should be the default — because what we observed looks more like a software engineering problem than a compute problem. cs already has years of tools that map onto these failure modes. the models may have just gotten good enough to use them. the article has everything: every score, every error class with tape examples, every fix, the full benchmark harness with all 80 questions, and the complete telegram bot code. run it yourself, swap in a different model, or just talk to the live bot — raw model, no fixes, warts and all.

we don't know how far this extends beyond mt-bench or whether the "correct reasoning, wrong commit" pattern has a name. we're sharing because we think more people should be looking at it. everything is open. the code is in the article. tear it apart.

fredmendoza 2026/04/16 02:54
弗雷德，很高兴认识你。评分模型不知道正在测试什么。我们使用单独的帐户来划分。克劳德分级机最终猜测是 GPT-3.5 Turbo 或 GPT-4。在编码块上，它始终将响应评分为 GPT-4o 水平。我们遵循创建团队发布的 MT-Bench 评分指南。按照书上的研究，没有马参加比赛。每个分数和每个回答都会发布在磁带中，因此如果您愿意，您可以自己重新评分整个内容。这是我打字的内容，我只是洛杉矶的一个人，花了一个周末通过 2B 模型运行 80 个问题，并认为结果很有趣，值得分享。
Fred, nice to meet you. The grading model had no idea what was being tested. We used separate accounts to compartmentalize. The Claude grader was guessing GPT-3.5 Turbo or GPT-4 by the end. On the coding block it consistently scored responses as GPT-4o level. We followed the MT-Bench grading guidelines as published by the team that created them. Did the research, followed the book, had no horse in the race. Every score and every response is published in the tape so you can regrade the whole thing yourself if you want. And this is me typing, I'm just a guy in LA who spent a weekend running 80 questions through a 2B model and thought the results were interesting enough to share.
